sacco

Class Turtle

  • java.lang.Object
    • sacco.SaccoWindow
      • sacco.Turtle
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    DrawingBoard, KeyboardListener, MouseListener

    public class Turtle
extends SaccoWindow

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Turtle()
      Constructs a default Turtle with a 500 x 500 window
      Turtle(int w, int h)
      Constructs a default Turtle in a window with the given width and height

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void clear()
      Clears all drawing in the background, leaving a white window.
      double getAngle()
      returns the current angle that the Turtle is facing
      int getTimeDelay() 
      double getX()
      returns the current x value of the Turtle
      double getY()
      returns the current y value of the Turtle
      void hideTurtle()
      Makes the Turtle invisible on the screen.
      boolean isPenDown() 
      void move(double distance)
      Moves the Turtle the distance passed in as a parameter
      void paintWindow(Canvas brush)
      Used internally to paint the Turtle and its drawing
      void penDown()
      Puts the Turtle's pen down.
      void penUp()
      Lifts the Turtle's pen up.
      void setColor(java.awt.Color col)
      Sets the color of the line drawn by the Turtle
      void setColor(int r, int g, int b)
      Sets the color of the line drawn using RGB values
      void setColor(java.lang.String col)
      Sets the color of the line drawn by the Turtle
      void setThickness(int thickness)
      Sets the thickness of the line drawn by the Turtle.
      void setTimeDelay(int milliSec)
      Sets the time delay between each of the Turtle's actions
      void showTurtle()
      Makes the Turtle visible on the screen
      void turnLeft(double degree)
      Turns the Turtle counter-clockwise the number of degrees passed as a parameter
      void turnRight(double degree)
      Turns the Turtle clockwise the number of degrees passed as a parameter

        addButton, centerOnScreen, exitOnClose, getGridSpacing, getHeight, getLocation, getWidth, hideGrid, hideWindow, launchSideCar, onButtonPress, onKeyPress, onKeyRelease, onMouseMove, onMousePress, onMouseRelease, onTimerTick, paint, paintGrid, paintToFile, pauseFor, quickSideCar, setDelay, setGridSpacing, setResizable, setSideCarVisibility, setSize, setTitle, setVisible, showGrid, showWindow, start, stop

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Turtle

        public Turtle()
        Constructs a default Turtle with a 500 x 500 window

      • Turtle

        public Turtle(int w,
      int h)
        Constructs a default Turtle in a window with the given width and height
        Parameters:
        w - The width of the Turtle's window
        h - The height of the Turtle's window

    • Method Detail

      • paintWindow

        public void paintWindow(Canvas brush)
        Used internally to paint the Turtle and its drawing
        Overrides:
        paintWindow in class SaccoWindow

      • getX

        public double getX()
        returns the current x value of the Turtle
        Returns:

      • getY

        public double getY()
        returns the current y value of the Turtle
        Returns:

      • isPenDown

        public boolean isPenDown()

      • penDown

        public void penDown()
        Puts the Turtle's pen down. All move commands will draw a line on the window

      • penUp

        public void penUp()
        Lifts the Turtle's pen up. When the Turtle moves, no lines will be drawn

      • showTurtle

        public void showTurtle()
        Makes the Turtle visible on the screen

      • hideTurtle

        public void hideTurtle()
        Makes the Turtle invisible on the screen.

      • turnLeft

        public void turnLeft(double degree)
        Turns the Turtle counter-clockwise the number of degrees passed as a parameter
        Parameters:
        degree - The number of degrees the Turtle will turn

      • turnRight

        public void turnRight(double degree)
        Turns the Turtle clockwise the number of degrees passed as a parameter
        Parameters:
        degree - The number of degrees the Turtle will turn

      • getTimeDelay

        public int getTimeDelay()

      • setColor

        public void setColor(java.lang.String col)
        Sets the color of the line drawn by the Turtle
        Parameters:
        col - One of the following: “red”,”black”,”white”,”blue” ,”green” ,”orange” ,”pink” ,or ”yellow”

      • setColor

        public void setColor(java.awt.Color col)
        Sets the color of the line drawn by the Turtle
        Parameters:
        col - A Color object representing the desired color of the line

      • setThickness

        public void setThickness(int thickness)
        Sets the thickness of the line drawn by the Turtle. Default is 1.
        Parameters:
        thickness -

      • getAngle

        public double getAngle()
        returns the current angle that the Turtle is facing
        Returns:

      • move

        public void move(double distance)
        Moves the Turtle the distance passed in as a parameter
        Parameters:
        distance - the distance that the Turtle will move

      • setColor

        public void setColor(int r,
            int g,
            int b)
        Sets the color of the line drawn using RGB values
        Parameters:
        r -
        g -
        b -

      • clear

        public void clear()
        Clears all drawing in the background, leaving a white window.

      • setTimeDelay

        public void setTimeDelay(int milliSec)
        Sets the time delay between each of the Turtle's actions
        Parameters:
        milliSec - The time delay in milliseconds (1000 ms = 1 sec)
